errors.title: Outdated Manim Feature
errors.what: The AI used a Manim feature that no longer exists in the current version.
errors.why: ParametricSurface was removed from Manim in v0.17 and replaced with Surface(). The AI learned from old examples.
errors.action: Click 'Try Again' — the validator will auto-fix this automatically.

errors.title: Wrong Scene Type
errors.what: The animation tried to move the camera but used the wrong scene type.
errors.why: Camera movement requires MovingCameraScene instead of Scene. The AI made a mistake choosing the base class.
errors.action: Click 'Try Again' — the validator now catches this automatically.

errors.title: Unknown Manim Feature
errors.what: The AI used a Manim class or function that doesn't exist.
errors.why: The AI invented or misremembered a Manim feature name. This is a known limitation of AI code generation.
errors.action: Click 'Try Again' to generate fresh code. If it keeps failing, try rephrasing your prompt to be more specific about what you want to see.

errors.title: Code Syntax Error
errors.what: The generated code has a Python syntax error and cannot run.
errors.why: The AI produced malformed code — this sometimes happens with complex requests.
errors.action: Click 'Try Again'. If you edited the code yourself, check for missing colons, brackets, or quotes in the Code tab.

errors.title: LaTeX Error
errors.what: The math equation renderer (LaTeX) failed to process a formula.
errors.why: Either LaTeX is not properly installed, a required package is missing, or the formula contains invalid syntax.
errors.action: Open MiKTeX Console and click 'Check for updates' to install missing packages. Then try again. If the problem persists, report this to the developer.

errors.title: Render Timed Out
errors.what: The animation took too long to render and was stopped.
errors.why: Complex animations take more time at higher quality settings. The current quality setting may be too high for this animation.
errors.action: Try a lower quality setting (480p or 720p) and render again. If the animation is very complex, it may just need more time.

errors.title: Engine Offline
errors.what: The rendering engine is not running.
errors.why: The Python engine that processes and renders animations has stopped or was never started.
errors.action: Double-click START.bat in your project folder to restart everything. The green dot in the bottom bar will turn on when it's ready.

errors.title: AI Quota Exhausted
errors.what: The AI service has reached its usage limit for today.
errors.why: Free AI APIs have daily limits. All available providers are currently at their limit.
errors.action: Wait a few minutes and try again — limits reset frequently. Or add your own free Groq API key in Settings for unlimited generations.

errors.title: Invalid Animation Structure
errors.what: The generated code is missing required parts to be a valid animation.
errors.why: Every Manim animation needs a class that extends Scene and a construct() method. The AI omitted one of these.
errors.action: Click 'Try Again' for a fresh attempt.

errors.title: Invalid Property
errors.what: The AI gave an invalid property to a Manim shape or object.
errors.why: Different Manim objects accept different properties. The AI used one that doesn't apply here (e.g. width= on a Circle).
errors.action: Click 'Try Again' — the validator will catch this automatically.

errors.pattern: No output file found|No mp4 found
errors.title: No Video Produced
errors.what: The animation ran but no video file was created.
errors.why: The animation may have had no visible content, or an issue occurred during video encoding.
errors.action: Make sure the animation has at least one self.play() call with a visible object. Try again or simplify your prompt.

errors.title: Unsupported Plot Type
errors.what: The AI tried to create an implicit function plot which Manim does not support.
errors.why: Manim can only plot y=f(x) functions and parametric curves. Implicit equations like f(x,y)=0 require a different approach.
errors.action: Click 'Try Again'. The validator will now catch this. Or rephrase your prompt — instead of 'plot x²+y²=5' try 'draw a circle with radius √5'.
